notes: Played single player, was able to browse multiplayer servers but any of the servers with Battleye protection are unavailable as this process will install but will fail to run.  I only played single player, but as this is also hosted on a server the non-official servers should offer similar performances. I started a new game with a new character and experienced no major performance issues. Performance would be on par with my Windows VM even with my out dated video drivers (I will update them before my next report)
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Major graphical glitches, placeable objects don't render at all. only launches with BattleEye disabled.
title: Conan Exiles

notes:
Graphics were incredibly pixelated - Fixed by toggling Low End Laptop mode. 
Creating a singleplayer game or browsing servers didn't work - Fixed by upgrading proton.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Can't start single player game. Several crashes.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: First of all you are stuck in low end laptop mode unless you like seeing DLSS type garbage on the screen. Secondly the thread management in this game is horrid on Windows, worse in Linux as it will crash as it tries to calculate the damage in combat. This is due to bad thread handling that is more on Funcom Oslo part to correct not Steam's Proton. Cutscreens also run with the same thread handling issue that stutters and hangs the game. With my Vega 64, it should be fine but the trash CPU management has this game nearing borked.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Battleye installation failed, but I was able to log to non-battleye server and played maybe 4 hours without issues.  Some times there was some stutter, but nothing horrible.  Performance was ok (eventhough graphics were very pixelated at the beginning).  I'd expect once they solve Battleye it will be perfectly playable.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Ran relatively flawlessly as long as you don't try to run with BattleEye.  I couldn't get that to work at all.  Otherwise, I did not have to do any tweaks to get logged in to a solo game.  Graphics looked great and I didn't notice any graphic or performance issues.  At least, once I changed the game over to window mode because it kept trying to put the fullscreen game on the wrong display (i have 3).  Only marking silver since I can't join regular online games due to lack of BattleEye.  I'm sure I can make a private server though, but haven't tried yet.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Its work very well on medium settings. I played in coop with my friend without battleye.  Proton 3.16.8 fixed problem with "pixel graphic" and at this moment its not neccesary to change graphic option to "Low spec laptop"
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Game worked 100% besides battleye (so dont use that) and the weird pixelated effect which went away after I changed the game to windowed, Was able to keep a solid 60fps at 2560x1080 with no lag spikes.

notes: I have played the game without use battleye (single player). Put fullscreen in a window to have the real fullscreen mode...
title: Conan Exiles

notes:
Runs great in both multiplayer and single-player with and without common mods, but only if you run it without Battleye - meaning you cannot get into official servers, or any other servers running Battleye.
You may have to tweak some of the gfx settings to get a good frame-rate - some of the god-rays and whatever other advanced gfx settings can cause abnormal frame-drops.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: I can log into the game without any issues.  I can connect to any server without BattleEye.  However, when I log into the game, the game has an odd shadow effect that blinks rapidly, on and off.  This effect persists even when the game is in windowed mode and in low-end laptop mode.  You can play the game this way, but it is very distracting.  Also, there is heavy stuttering that happens whenever a movement is made with the character.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: just dont play on battle eye servers games runs perfect
title: Conan Exiles

notes: I have played the game using Proton for 235 hours, it has crashed a grand total of maybe 4 times in that entire time. Everything works out of the box, all graphical options. I have a bunch of mods installed via Steam Workshop and they all work fine. I don't have Windows to compare performance, but it performs plenty well enough for my satisfaction.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Could run really smootly but very big stutters occur quite regularly with hard disk imprint. I feel it could be related to the textures streaming part but I don't know what to look for. I found nothing in logs. ( BattlEye obviously not installed)
title: Conan Exiles

notes:
I gave this a Gold Rating exclusively for Single-Player, as I was able to run the game at the same quality and perfomance as on windows. I run an amd build which has a known issue related to sound. This is fixed by adding the below script to the engine.ini file found in your .steam/steamapps/conan exiles/config flolder. That will clean up the stutter associated with amd cpu and audio issues.

[/script/engine.audiosettings] 
bDisableMasterEQ=True

Battle Eye has not been tested but the single player game receives a Gold Status as it requires tweaks, but can be played on equal par with Windows.
title: Conan Exiles

Crashes after pressing main menu buttons, can't play single- or multiplayer
Error popup: "Fatal error please send crash report". Tried to Disable D3d11, D3d10, Esync and Use wine D9vk but still crashes.
title: Conan Exiles

notes:
It doesn't work at all after being automatically updated to 4.11-2. After the main menu is displayed, any menu selection will cause a “fatal error” to be terminated. 
When using 4.11-1, it worked without any major problems. The trouble is flickering when the guilt is displayed at the end of character creation (it does not occur during the game). Since the entire screen is blurred when it is first started, it is necessary to change to the resolution you want after switching to laptop mode.
Now, I play games with 4.2-9 (it works perfectly fine with 4.2-9).
title: Conan Exiles

notes:
Proton 4.11.2 crashes as other has reported. 
Proton 4.9 works without a problem. Got over 200 hours without any crashes with 4.9. Missing support for BattleEye excludes alot of servers though.

title: Conan Exiles

notes: The game works great out of the box, but only with BattleEye disabled. I recommend to set "Fullscreen in Window". 60 fps on medium graphics settings with 2K screen resolution. Antialising disabled.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Game runs great out of the box with Battleeye disabled. runs very smooth on ultra settings. Battleeye disabled means no official servers, but there are plenty of other servers available. Also I installed the Conan Dedicated server on a seperate machine running Ubuntu and it runs perfectly under wine also.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: Came runs fine single player, surprisingly stable, no crashes. Had some very bad frame stutter but after switching the install to SSD, it's pretty smooth 60fps on Ultra. If it wasn't for BattleEye, I'd give it Platinum. Dedicated Server seems to work as well.
title: Conan Exiles

notes: No working logo videos (UE4 and Nvidia) and no BattlEye support which means you can not play on official and unofficial servers which have BattlEye enabled. Coop and playing on non BattlEye servers works like a charm. Otherwise this game would be a Platinum rating because no tweaks are necessary, the performance is excellent and the game run even more stable and faster than on Windows
title: Conan Exiles
